findings.description: Rule 4 was introduced with 'unique within the AC file' wording. Commit a428840 (2026-05-14) fixed the same rule in spec-template/SKILL.md but did NOT touch story-planner.md. story-planner.md:163 is the remaining outlier.
story.id and story_order patterns remain '^[0-9]{2}$' — never changed since introduction. All on-disk stories.json use S-prefix format (S1, S2). Schema is the outlier.
Does not exist. Only stories.schema.json and result.schema.json are in plugins/base/schemas/. Epic may optionally create it.

Old verification.json shape uses 'id'/'type'/'check_method'/'status' — the richer task-specific shape from before story-planner was updated. Newer epics (base-next) use 'question_id'/'category'. The current prompt already uses the new shape.

explorers.notes: The exact Rule 4 fix is a single-sentence edit mirroring spec-template/SKILL.md:243. The stories.schema.json pattern fix affects lines 39 and 79 only. verification.schema.json is net-new.

findings.description: plugins/base/ has four subdirs: agents/ (agent prompts), commands/ (user commands), schemas/ (JSON Schema files), skills/ (skill SKILL.md + supporting files). schemas/ has exactly 2 files: stories.schema.json and result.schema.json.

feature.md orchestrates story-planner in all three modes. It reads stories.json story_order entries as strings — already handles S-prefix in production. No prose changes needed in feature.md from this epic.
Consumes verification.json questions keyed by question_id (VQ-S1-001 format). Category, phase, ac_id are all read. VQ examples in the prompt use S-prefix story IDs — consistent with real files.
Reads verification.json pre-impl questions, appends post-impl questions. Enforces immutability of pre-impl questions. post-impl records must also conform to any new schema.

explorers.notes: The epic's changes are isolated to story-planner.md, stories.schema.json, and optionally verification.schema.json (new). No changes needed in feature.md, verification-examiner.md, or integration-architect.md — they already handle the S-prefix convention. story_order consumer chain is schema-only; no prose updates needed downstream.

findings.description: No automated test suite. Validation is prose ACs with embedded shell commands (grep, python3 -m json.tool). The only build script is `make release` → `node scripts/release.mjs`.
Commit convention: Conventional Commits with scope. `fix(base):`, `feat(base):`, `refactor(base):`, `chore(base):`, `spec:`. Release commits use 'Release base v2.x.y'.
Schema files use JSON Schema Draft 2020-12. Referenced by path from agent prompts. No ajv or validation tooling wired to any runner.

Story IDs in every on-disk stories.json use S-prefix (S1, S2, S3). Confirmed in epic-base-next/stories.json, epic-lean-lead-decider/stories.json, epic-next-modes/stories.json. The ^[0-9]{2}$ schema pattern has never matched real production artifacts.

explorers.notes: No language-specific build system applies. The project is a Markdown/JSON documentation system. All validation is manual via prose AC shell commands executed by agents.
